signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Positioning
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.94 continues to test higher, driven by the significant +266bp Fed-BoJ carry premium (SOFR 3.64% vs TONA 0.98%). JPY COT LF net short −115,033 (long%=28.4%) indicates crowded speculative short positioning, making it vulnerable to a short squeeze. The BoJ's recent hike to 1.0% and 'Hold' bias from OIS suggests a shift in policy, increasing intervention risk above 162.00. Watch for any BoJ verbal intervention or a sustained break above 162.00 to trigger further short covering.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail Extreme
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −23,314 (long%=21.6%) indicates a crowded speculative short position, diverging from the RBNZ's 'Hike' bias with a hike probability priced for the next meeting. Retail sentiment is at an extreme 94% long, creating a strong institutional vs. retail divergence. If the RBNZ delivers or signals a hike at the 8 Jul meeting, a significant short-squeeze risk is elevated, targeting 0.5700+. NZD/USD currently at 0.5653.

signals.title: EUR/AUD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: EUR is the strongest G10 currency this week with an average +0.463% performance across 7 pairs, while AUD is the weakest at -0.387% across 4 pairs, creating a significant 0.850% spread. This divergence occurs despite AUD having over USD (AONIA 4.35% vs SOFR 3.64%), suggesting fundamental weakness in AUD. The EUR's relative strength is notable given its moderate negative CESI score. Watch for AUD data releases to confirm or reverse this underperformance.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail Extreme + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/CAD retail sentiment shows an extreme 93% short bias, indicating potential contrarian reversal risk. This is occurring as the USD/CAD vs WTI Oil correlation breaks significantly to -0.743 (z=-1.96σ vs norm=-0.027), suggesting CAD is not benefiting from the WTI Oil rebound (+2.20% today) as it historically would. The pair is at 1.4202, consolidating around 1.4210. Watch for a sustained break below 1.4200 to invalidate the retail contrarian read.
